title: Aruba 10-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Aruba 10-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 10-day trip to Aruba, Aruba: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Arikok National Park guided jeep tour and De Palm Island day pass. Prices from $1984 per person (estimate).
highlights: Arikok National Park guided jeep tour
4x4 Arikok National Park tour to the Natural Pool
Private catamaran sunset sail with open bar
Dinner with your feet in the sand at Flying Fishbone
overview: Spend 10 days discovering Aruba, Aruba — Arid tropical climate, warm and remarkably stable year-round (highs 30-32°C) with near-constant trade winds and low rainfall — one of the driest islands in the Caribbean. Aruba sits outside the hurricane belt, so it's a reliable year-round destination; January to April offers the driest, breeziest weather and the busiest season. This itinerary blends Arikok National Park guided jeep tour, De Palm Island day pass, Eagle Beach with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
9 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Arikok National Park guided jeep tour, De Palm Island day pass, Eagle Beach
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Aruba
itinerary.description: Land at Queen Beatrix International Airport (Oranjestad) (AUA) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Arikok National Park guided jeep tour — Desert landscape, caves and the Natural Pool (Conchi) by 4x4. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.
activities: Arikok National Park guided jeep tour

itinerary.title: Aruba: De Palm Island day pass & Butterfly Farm
itinerary.description: Morning visit to De Palm Island day pass — Private island with water slides, snorkeling and a pool. In the afternoon, continue to Butterfly Farm — Walk-through tropical butterfly garden. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Aruba: Aruba Ostrich Farm tour & Atlantis Submarine Expedition
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Aruba Ostrich Farm tour — Guided tour and ostrich encounters. In the afternoon, continue to Atlantis Submarine Expedition — Real submarine descent to view reefs and a shipwreck. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Arikok National Park full-island tour
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Arikok National Park full-island tour, a popular side trip from Aruba. Return to Aruba in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Arikok National Park full-island tour

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Conchi Natural Pool in Arikok National Park — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.
activities: Conchi Natural Pool in Arikok National Park

itinerary.title: Aruba: California Lighthouse area horseback ride & Jolly Pirates snorkel and sunset sail
itinerary.description: Morning visit to California Lighthouse area horseback ride — Guided coastal horseback riding tour. In the afternoon, continue to Jolly Pirates snorkel and sunset sail — Catamaran cruise with snorkeling and rope swing. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Jolly Pirates snorkel and sunset sail

itinerary.title: Day Trip to San Nicolas street art district
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to San Nicolas street art district, a popular side trip from Aruba. Return to Aruba in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: San Nicolas street art district

itinerary.title: Aruba: UTV/ATV Arikok backcountry tour & Bushiribana Gold Mill Ruins guided stop
itinerary.description: Morning visit to UTV/ATV Arikok backcountry tour — Off-road tour through the national park's desert terrain. In the afternoon, continue to Bushiribana Gold Mill Ruins guided stop — Historic 19th-century gold smelting ruins, usually part of an island tour. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Bushiribana Gold Mill Ruins guided stop

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include San Nicolas street art murals — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Casibari Rock Formation climb or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to AUA for your departure flight.

travelTips: Complete the mandatory online ED-card before departure — it's required for entry and check-in.
Rent a 4x4 or book a jeep tour to properly explore Arikok National Park and the Natural Pool — regular cars can't handle the terrain.
Aruba sits outside the hurricane belt, making it one of the more weather-reliable Caribbean picks even in summer/fall.
Book a sunset catamaran cruise — Aruba's consistent trade winds make for excellent sailing conditions.
Try Zeerovers in Savaneta for a genuinely local, inexpensive fresh-fish experience away from resort strips.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Strong reef-safe sunscreen — the arid climate means intense, near-constant sun
A light windbreaker for the island's steady trade winds
Sturdy shoes for Arikok National Park's rocky desert trails
Light, breathable clothing given the consistently warm, dry heat
thingsToDo: Arikok National Park guided jeep tour
Jolly Pirates snorkel and sunset sail
Bushiribana Gold Mill Ruins guided stop
Aruba Aloe Museum and Factory tour
thingsToAvoid: Swimming or driving in Arikok's Natural Pool area during rough surf — conditions can turn dangerous quickly.
Underestimating the sun — the dry trade winds mask how strong the UV exposure actually is.
Renting a regular sedan for national park exploration — much of it requires 4x4 access.
Leaving valuables visible in rental cars at trailheads or beach parking areas.
Assuming the US dollar is the only accepted currency — the Aruban florin is standard, though USD is widely accepted too.
topRestaurants: Flying Fishbone — Seafood, tables in the sand ($$$$)
Taste My Aruba — Local Aruban Food Tour ($$)
shoppingAreas: Caya G.F. Betico Croes shopping street
nightlife: Casino at the Renaissance Aruba Resort
instagramSpots: Eagle Beach fofoti trees at sunset
hiddenGems: Conchi Natural Pool in Arikok National Park
familyActivities: De Palm Island water park day
adventureActivities: 4x4 Arikok National Park tour to the Natural Pool
luxuryExperiences: Private catamaran sunset sail with open bar
Fine dining at Papiamento or Flying Fishbone
Suite stay at a Palm Beach high-rise resort
Private day pass to Renaissance Island
honeymoonExperiences: Dinner with your feet in the sand at Flying Fishbone
Sunset horseback ride near the California Lighthouse
Couples spa day overlooking Palm Beach
budgetExperiences: Free public access to Eagle Beach
Climb the free Casibari Rock Formations
Fresh grilled fish at Zeerovers for a few dollars

festivalCalendar.name: Aruba Carnival
festivalCalendar.timing: January to early March, culminating before Lent

festivalCalendar.name: Dera Gai (Bury the Rooster) folklore festival

localEtiquette: Locals speak Papiamento, Dutch and English fluently — a friendly "Bon bini" (welcome) or "Danki" (thank you) is appreciated.
Tipping 10-15% is standard; some restaurants add a service charge automatically — check the bill.
Aruba is known as "One Happy Island" — a relaxed, friendly demeanor is the local norm and expected of visitors too.
Beachwear is for the beach only; cover up when entering restaurants, shops or Oranjestad town.
Dutch influence means punctuality for tours and reservations is taken more seriously than on some neighboring islands.
localTransportGuide: Arubus public buses connect the airport, Oranjestad and the main hotel strips cheaply, but a rental car or taxi is far more practical for reaching Arikok National Park, the north coast and San Nicolas. Taxis have government-set fixed rates by zone rather than meters.
bestTimeToVisit: Aruba sits outside the hurricane belt, so it's a reliable year-round destination; January to April offers the driest, breeziest weather and the busiest season.
weather: Arid tropical climate, warm and remarkably stable year-round (highs 30-32°C) with near-constant trade winds and low rainfall — one of the driest islands in the Caribbean.
visaInformation: Visa-free entry for most Western passport holders for tourist stays up to 90 days, plus a mandatory online ED-card (Embarkation/Disembarkation card) before arrival. Indian passport holders typically need a valid US, UK, Canadian or Schengen visa to qualify for visa-free entry, or otherwise a visa arranged in advance — check current rules before booking.
averageFlightTime: ~16.3h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Aruba?
faqs.answer: Visa-free entry for most Western passport holders for tourist stays up to 90 days, plus a mandatory online ED-card (Embarkation/Disembarkation card) before arrival. Indian passport holders typically need a valid US, UK, Canadian or Schengen visa to qualify for visa-free entry, or otherwise a visa arranged in advance — check current rules before booking.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Aruba?
faqs.answer: Aruba sits outside the hurricane belt, so it's a reliable year-round destination; January to April offers the driest, breeziest weather and the busiest season. Arid tropical climate, warm and remarkably stable year-round (highs 30-32°C) with near-constant trade winds and low rainfall — one of the driest islands in the Caribbean.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Aruba safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Aruba is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Aruba?
faqs.answer: The local currency is AWG. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Aruba is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Dinner with your feet in the sand at Flying Fishbone experience. It also works well for families — highlights include De Palm Island water park day.

reviews.author: Priya Patel
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Eagle Beach in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Mia Patel
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Casibari Rock Formation climb and Alto Vista Chapel. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Sarah Brown
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Aruba is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Casibari Rock Formation climb. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Mohammed Andersen
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Atlantis Submarine Expedition alone was worth the trip, but combined with Baby Beach this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Marco Müller
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Baby Beach and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.
